Name: Precisely targeted neuromodulation of opto-genetic data sets with blue and green light interleaved stimuluation post virus injected at S2
Data Description: For the spine, a custom-built saddle-shaped transmit–receive surface coil (2.5 × 3 cm2 in size) was positioned over each animal’s neck. Five contiguous axial image slices (3 mm thickness) covering C3-C7 cervical segments, with the third slice positioned over the C5 segment, were acquired in each imaging session. High-resolution structural gradient echo images (TR = 220 ms, TE = 3.24 ms, FOV = 32×32 mm2, matrix size = 128×128, in-plane resolution = 0.25 × 0.25 mm2, thickness = 3 mm, 5 slices) with magnetization transfer contrast (MTC) were acquired following a Gaussian saturation pulse (flip angle = 820°, pulse width = 12 ms, RF offset = 5,000 Hz). Multiple runs of stimulus - evoked fMRI data were acquired using the same block-design stimulation protocol as applied for brain fMRI. BOLD fMRI data were acquired using a fast gradient echo sequence (flip angle = ~18°, TR = 46.88 ms, TE = 6.5 ms, in-plane resolution = 0.5 × 0.5 mm2, 3s per volume, 250 volumes) covering the same geometry as anatomic imaging. Pre-process and codes used to plot data with instructions are available on github repository (https://github.com/arimishra/repositoryPNAS)
